What published NLH essays are about

All 288 essays averaged into one — recurring themes by share of corpus mass. This is the subject-matter recipe. Click any bar for the theme and its essays.

Footnote: what the 3 prize-winners over-weight (N=3 — a side question, not the recipe)

The prize is a different, smaller signal than publication. Over-index = winner-centroid ÷ corpus mean.

How a published NLH essay moves

Each sixth of the essay, and the move-atoms that peak there. Read top to bottom for the corpus's rhetorical progression — where it opens, develops, and closes.

Walk an essay across the corpus

Pick any of the 288 published essays and see where it sits against the recipe: the themes it leans into versus the corpus, how its rhetorical arc tracks the typical opening → close, and the essays nearest to it. Click a neighbor to walk there.
